mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present equity values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following low FEDFUNDS levels below 0.25% are not significantly higher than long-run baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines below 6.5%, the economy crosses a threshold into self-sustaining expansion, reducing equity risk premia and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE is below 6.5%, forward 60-day returns are negative in greater than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts reveal a synchronized demand acceleration across consumption and construction, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST rise month-over-month, forward 5-day returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate policy environment, accelerating inflation captured by a rising consumer price index signals imminent monetary policy normalization, causing equity markets to front-run tightening and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month, forward 20-day returns are positive in greater than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side efficiency gains, which translate into positive earnings revisions and drive S&P 500 returns higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 3-month momentum in INDPRO shows a Rank IC below 0.04 with 60-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate signals a broad-based labor market recovery that boosts disposable income and consumption, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases simultaneously, forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,000,000 units signals a durable recovery in residential construction that generates positive spillovers into consumer durable spending and employment, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods where HOUST exceeds 1000, forward 20-day returns are negative in greater than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production crossing above 103 indicates that manufacturing activity has recovered to a level sufficient to support operating leverage and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When INDPRO is above 103, forward 5-day returns are negative in greater than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.
